nodes.name: verification harness
nodes.description: A harness that makes verification of code behavior explicit, reducing dependence on human review.

nodes.name: human review
nodes.description: Manual verification process that models writing code rely on when they cannot verify their own behavior.

nodes.name: project memory
nodes.description: A memory facility provided to agents whose use must be inspectable and auditable to be useful. | A harness component providing contextual guidance to help models localize files in the codebase | Component introduced at H2 for persisting project-level state across interactions. | 跨回合/会话的项目级记忆存储。 | A critical runtime resource maintained by the harness to preserve state and history across agent operations. | A harness component that weaker models depend heavily on for localizing files.

nodes.name: context trace
nodes.description: A record that traces which memory artifact was consulted, what it contributed, and when, enabling auditability of memory use.

nodes.name: agent
nodes.description: A software entity (e.g., a code-writing model) whose behavior and memory use must be auditable. | 在 H0-H3 harness ladder 中接受逐步增加运行时支持的对象。 | 在该框架语境下，目标用户从人类开发者转变为能够在仓库上可靠工作的智能体。 | 运行在 harness 之上的执行主体，其观察、行动、反馈接收和完成判定等运行时行为均由 harness 治理。 | 在 harness–environment 系统中执行任务的智能体，其行为（如写入逻辑、检查文件、选择测试、应用补丁）是自主性差距的具象化载体。 | The AI agent that is mediated and constrained by the harness, performing context selection, tool invocation, and verification actions. | 该框架的分析对象，能够编写逻辑、选择测试、应用补丁并进行验证的执行实体 | The entity whose context selection, tool use, failure interpretation, and behavior verification are shaped by the harness.

nodes.name: arXiv:2107.03374
nodes.description: arXiv预印本论文，2021年发布，编号2107.03374。

nodes.name: Language Models are Few-Shot Learners
nodes.description: 由Tom B. Brown等人于2020年发表在NeurIPS的论文，介绍GPT-3模型，展示了大语言模型的少样本学习能力。 | GPT-3 论文《Language models are few-shot learners》,由 Tom B. Brown 等人发表,展示了大规模语言模型在少样本学习任务上的能力

nodes.name: SWE-bench
nodes.description: 由Carlos E. Jimenez等人于2024年发表在ICLR的论文，评估语言模型解决真实GitHub问题的能力。 | 发表于 ICLR 2024 的论文，提出用于评估语言模型解决真实 GitHub issue 能力的基准测试。

nodes.name: NeurIPS
nodes.description: 神经信息处理系统大会（Advances in Neural Information Processing Systems），顶级机器学习学术会议。 | Advances in Neural Information Processing Systems，顶级人工智能学术会议，SWE-agent 的发表 venue | Advances in Neural Information Processing Systems，国际顶级机器学习会议，SWE-agent 论文发表于此。 | Advances in Neural Information Processing Systems - the venue where both papers were published

nodes.name: ICLR
nodes.description: 国际学习表征会议（International Conference on Learning Representations），深度学习领域重要会议。 | International Conference on Learning Representations，机器学习领域的顶级学术会议。 | International Conference on Learning Representations，国际顶级机器学习学术会议。 | International Conference on Learning Representations，国际顶级机器学习会议，SWE-bench 论文发表于此。 | International Conference on Learning Representations, the venue where AgentBench was published in 2024. | International Conference on Learning Representations, a top machine learning venue where OpenHands was published.

nodes.name: Chain-of-Thought Prompting
nodes.description: Wei et al., 2022, NeurIPS. 提出通过思维链提示激发大语言模型的推理能力。

nodes.name: ReAct
nodes.description: Yao et al., 2023, ICLR. 提出在语言模型中协同结合推理（Reasoning）与行动（Acting）的方法。 | 论文 'ReAct: Synergizing reasoning and acting in language models'，发表于 ICLR 2023，提出在语言模型中协同推理与行动的方法。

nodes.name: AutoGen
nodes.description: Wu, Bansal, Zhang, Wu 等作者署名的论文（切片中被截断），为多智能体对话框架相关研究。 | 微软等团队提出的多智能体对话框架，发表于 COLM 2024，通过多智能体对话支持下一代 LLM 应用。 | 由 Wu、White、Doug Burger 和 Chi Wang 提出的多智能体对话框架，用于实现下一代 LLM 应用，发表于 COLM 2024。

nodes.name: Chain-of-Thought Reasoning
nodes.description: 通过显式生成中间推理步骤来提升大语言模型推理能力的技术。

nodes.name: Reasoning and Acting
nodes.description: 在语言模型中将推理与行动相结合的范式，是 ReAct 论文的核心思想。

nodes.name: NeurIPS 2022
nodes.description: Chain-of-Thought 论文发表的会议：Advances in Neural Information Processing Systems, 2022。

nodes.name: ICLR 2023
nodes.description: ReAct 论文发表的会议：International Conference on Learning Representations, 2023。

nodes.name: development harness
nodes.description: A runtime testing/debugging framework whose component responsibilities are enumerated by Table 1, covering failure modes like residue, test weakening, and boundary violations within the loop. | A runtime environment for agents that manages an analogous but distinct set of runtime resources compared to traditional operating systems.

nodes.name: Table 1
nodes.description: A reference table that enumerates the eleven component responsibilities of a development harness, mapping each responsibility to its runtime contract, characteristic failure mode, and evidence artifact.

nodes.name: component responsibility
nodes.description: An identifiable runtime decision or obligation within a development harness; there are eleven such responsibilities, each corresponding to a concrete runtime action rather than a framework-internal abstraction.

nodes.name: runtime contract
nodes.description: The behavioral obligation that each component responsibility of a development harness must fulfill during execution.

nodes.name: characteristic failure mode
nodes.description: The typical failure pattern (e.g., residue, test weakening, boundary violations) that arises when a specific component responsibility of the development harness is absent.

nodes.name: evidence artifact
nodes.description: A traceable record produced by a development harness responsibility that is captured within a recorded episode for later inspection. | The recorded output produced by each of the eleven responsibilities in a recorded episode of an agent-on-repository system.

nodes.name: recorded episode
nodes.description: A captured execution trace within the development harness in which evidence artifacts produced by component responsibilities are stored.

nodes.name: Trace Recording
nodes.description: 一种将代理运行过程以 JSONL 文件形式记录下来的方法体系，捕获外部有意义操作而非内部推理的每个 token

nodes.name: Action Trace
nodes.description: Trace Recording 中的一个组件，用于捕获代理外部有意义的操作序列 | Captures externally meaningful operations rather than every token of internal reasoning. | Record of model actions captured during an agent episode, included in the episode package. | 捕获所有代理操作序列的追踪类型，用于诊断整体 episode coherence

nodes.name: Tool Trace
nodes.description: Trace Recording 中的一个组件，记录每次命令调用，包括退出码、持续时间、超时状态和结果 | Records every command invocation with exit code, duration, timeout status, and recovery status. | Record of tool invocations and their results captured during an agent episode, included in the episode package. | 捕获 tool budget 和 test-time compute 等运行时资源的追踪类型，用于诊断 tool 类失败

nodes.name: JSONL
nodes.description: Trace Recording 所采用的文件格式，按行存储 JSON 对象 | 行结构化的 JSON 格式（JSON Lines），用于编码各类 trace 记录。 | Line-structured JSON format used as the compact schema for each trace type.

nodes.name: Evaluation Protocol
nodes.description: 评估流程中使用的协议，规定了评估过程的规范

nodes.name: Verification Report Template
nodes.description: 评估流程中使用的验证报告模板 | Template for verification reports provided to the H3-level agent configuration. | A template within H2 used to structure and present verification results.

nodes.name: Evaluator Pack
nodes.description: 包含评估者注释（如期望归因和期望修复）的独立评估包，对任何层级的代理都不可见 | Separate pack containing evaluator notes including expected attribution and expected fix, not visible to the agent at any level.

nodes.name: Task interface
nodes.description: 运行时契约组件，呈现任务的目标、需求、约束和成功标准。

nodes.name: Context manager
nodes.description: 运行时契约组件，负责选择并暴露与任务相关的项目内容。

nodes.name: Tool registry
nodes.description: 运行时契约组件，声明可用工具及允许执行的命令。 | Introduced at H1; defines the set of tools available to the agent. | 工具注册表，向智能体提供可用工具集合的组件，是 H1 层级的核心增强。 | 工具注册表，H1 引入的核心组件之一，用于显式化与追踪可用工具集合。 | 声明代理可用的工具及允许执行的命令列表，控制工具调用权限 | An artifact visible from H1 upward (hidden at H0). | H1 中引入的工具注册中心，用于管理 agent 可调用的工具集合。

nodes.name: Project memory
nodes.description: 运行时契约组件，提供项目级记忆信息（内容截断）。 | 项目记忆组件，用于在智能体执行过程中持久化项目相关状态信息。 | Stable, agent-readable project knowledge; missing it leads to repeated rediscovery and wrong-layer fixes. | 为代理提供架构、测试与已知失败知识的可读项目记忆 | 软件工程活动所依赖的关键支撑组件之一，用于维护项目状态和历史信息。 | 在长时程软件任务中跨episode保留的记忆，随时间可能变得有用或腐败（rot）。 | H2 中维护的项目级长期记忆模块。

nodes.name: Task record
nodes.description: 任务执行产生的证据工件，记录任务接口的运行轨迹。

nodes.name: Context trace
nodes.description: 上下文执行产生的证据工件，记录上下文管理器的运行轨迹。 | 记录代理所引用的项目记忆（project memory）等上下文信息的跟踪。 | 记录哪些项目记忆制品被查阅、它们贡献了什么，以及该贡献是否影响了代理决策 | 上下文追踪类型，关联上下文预算（context budget）与项目记忆（project memory）。 | 对环境上下文（如工作目录、文件路径、约束条件）的追踪记录

nodes.name: Tool trace
nodes.description: 工具执行产生的证据工件，记录工具注册表的运行轨迹。 | 记录工具调用相关信息的跟踪，包括命令、退出码、持续时间、超时状态、失败类型以及代理是否恢复。 | 记录命令、退出码、持续时间、超时状态、失败类型以及代理是否恢复 | 工具调用追踪类型，关联工具预算（tool budget）与测试时计算（test-time compute）。 | 对工具调用过程（参数、返回、超时与失败）的轨迹记录

nodes.name: Runtime contract
nodes.description: 框架在运行时强制执行的契约，由任务接口、上下文管理器、工具注册表、项目记忆等组件构成。

nodes.name: software-engineering agent
nodes.description: An autonomous agent designed to perform software-engineering tasks in realistic settings.

nodes.name: local benchmarks
nodes.description: Benchmarks used locally to evaluate models, which show a performance gap compared to realistic tasks.

nodes.name: realistic software tasks
nodes.description: Actual software-engineering tasks executed in real repository settings, used as the true measure of agent competence.

nodes.name: unit of analysis
nodes.description: The analytical choice of what entity (model alone vs. full system) to evaluate when interpreting agent behavior.

nodes.name: attribution error
nodes.description: The misattribution of success or failure to the model alone, ignoring the broader system context such as task representation and repository observation.

nodes.name: autonomous episode
nodes.description: A self-contained execution run of an autonomous agent, used as the granularity at which success or failure is observed.

nodes.name: repository
nodes.description: The code repository that the agent observes as a subset, serving as part of the operational context for autonomous episodes.

nodes.name: model
nodes.description: The underlying AI model that, in realistic settings, rarely acts alone but is often mistakenly treated as the sole unit of analysis. | AI 模型在真实环境中很少单独行动，需依赖运行时结构完成任务的表征、观察、调用工具与接收反馈等步骤 | 被 harness 所影响和约束行为的目标实体，但 harness 本身并非模型，仅作用于模型之上。

nodes.name: AI Harness Engineering
nodes.description: 介于基础模型与软件环境之间的中介层，负责协调上下文、动作、反馈与验证，将模型的潜在推理能力转化为在环境中可执行的行动 | A new research object defined in this paper, distinct from agent-computer interfaces and agent frameworks, focusing on the layer that manages context, tools, memory, and state for foundation-model software agents. | A runtime layer that surrounds a foundation-model software agent and manages context, tools, project memory, task state, observability, failure attribution, verification, permissions, and maintenance state. | An academic paper proposing a runtime substrate for foundation-model software agents, addressing unreliability of autonomous software-engineering agents in realistic development settings. | A proposed runtime substrate design philosophy aimed at improving the reliability of foundation-model software agents in realistic software development. | The missing layer for foundation-model agents, analogous to operating systems but specialized for managing context, tools, memory, verification, permissions, failure recovery, human oversight, entropy, cost, and risk. | 中介层，位于软件环境与自主软件工程能力之间，协调上下文、动作、反馈与验证证据。 | A new research object distinct from agent-computer interfaces, agent frameworks, and agent operating systems, identified with eleven component responsibilities and five design principles.

nodes.name: Model-Harness-Environment System
nodes.description: 由基础模型、AI Harness 与软件环境三部分构成的系统架构，用于驱动 AI 在软件环境中完成编码任务 | The combined system whose ability to produce a verifiable, attributed, maintainable change is the central concern of the framework.

nodes.name: Foundation Model
nodes.description: 系统中的模型层，提供潜在的推理与编码能力（latent reasoning and coding capability） | 具备代码、推理、规划和指令遵循等潜在能力的基础模型，是 AI 智能体的底层能力来源。 | 作为软件代理核心推理与生成能力的底层模型，是 autonomous software engineering 范式的基础组件。 | 具备代码生成、代码解释与补丁建议等代码能力的基础模型，是软件工程能力的底层支撑。

nodes.name: Software Environment
nodes.description: 系统中的环境层，提供代码仓库、测试、工具、日志与构建能力（repositories, tests, tools, logs, build affordances）

nodes.name: Episode Package
nodes.description: 一次完整执行周期的产物集合，包含 trace、验证报告、失败归因与熵审计（traces, verification report, failure attribution, entropy audit） | An auditable record produced by each evaluation episode, containing traces (action, tool, context, verification), intervention log, failure attribution, entropy audit, outcome record, and patch+verification report. | The output bundle produced by an agent episode, containing eight trace types, a patch, and a verification report. | An auditable output package produced from an agent run by the trace-based evaluation protocol, containing structured evidence.

nodes.name: Verification Report
nodes.description: AI Harness 输出的验证证据，用于评估任务执行结果 | The output produced in step 5 of the H3 verification workflow, documenting verification results and their limits. | 由 patch 与 trace 生成的验证报告，作为五标签最终结果分类法的输入 | A report where limitations of the agent's computed solution are documented. | Harness Engineering 中的结构化验证产物，用于记录确定性需求检查与失败归因的结果。

nodes.name: Failure Attribution
nodes.description: 对执行失败原因的归因分析，作为 episode package 的一部分 | Analysis attributing the cause of failures encountered during an agent episode, recorded in the episode package. | AI Development Harness 中用于定位和归因智能体失败原因的组件。 | Evidence element localizing the cause of agent failure, produced at the highest harness level. | A methodology that separates diagnosis from action in agent error handling, determining whether the agent ignored, misunderstood, or correctly applied available memory/resources. | Harness Engineering 中的关键机制之一，用于将执行失败定位归因到具体责任方（模型、harness 或环境）。 | 对智能体执行失败进行系统性归因的诊断过程，包含识别失败类型、收集证据及提出替代解释。 | A component of the agent harness that identifies and assigns failures within an agent's execution. | 将失败诊断与执行动作分离的实践，使得可以判断 agent 是忽略了、可误解地还是正确地应用了可用 memory。

nodes.name: Entropy Audit
nodes.description: 对执行过程不确定性或熵变化的审计记录，作为 episode package 的一部分 | Audit of randomness/uncertainty signals in the agent's behavior, recorded in the episode package. | An audit produced at the end of each episode that classifies any agent-introduced residue and uses a harness-gap label. | Produced at the end of each episode, it classifies any agent-introduced residue.

nodes.name: Agent
nodes.description: A software agent that performs software engineering tasks, including coding, reasoning, planning, and tool use. | The entity whose coherent and verifiable behavior is enabled by the development harness's resource management. | The autonomous entity whose operations, memory consultations, and verification attempts are recorded across the various trace components. | The execution entity bound by H3 to follow the five-step verification discipline. | 被工具追踪和上下文追踪观察与记录决策行为的主体 | An actor within the model–harness–environment system that is bound by H3 to produce requirement-linked evidence. | 本文研究对象中的软件智能体，是评估框架的执行主体。 | 在 H3 框架下运作的智能体组件，需承担复现失败、归因、应用修复、检查需求及报告等验证职责。 | 使用 memory 并根据上下文做出决策的智能体系统，是 context trace 与 failure attribution 的应用主体。 | An AI agent that must reproduce failures, attribute issues, apply targeted fixes, verify requirements, and report evidence and limitations. | 模型调用实体（智能体），无法像人类开发者那样通过社交化、文档和经验自然获取开发环境中的隐式支持。 | The software entity that is supported by the AI Harness and progressively exposed to runtime support via the H0-H3 ladder.

nodes.name: Runtime Support
nodes.description: The missing runtime layer responsible for identifying relevant context, explaining repository structure, selecting tools, interpreting feedback, enforcing architectural boundaries, verifying behavior, and cleaning up residue. | The missing operational assistance required by AI coding agents, supplied by humans to bridge gaps in context, tool selection, feedback interpretation, boundary enforcement, and verification.

nodes.name: Model Capability
nodes.description: The dominant framing that attributes agent failure on software tasks to insufficient capability in coding, reasoning, planning, or tool use, with the implied remedy being to train better models or compose them into more sophisticated agents.

nodes.name: Software Engineering Task
nodes.description: The class of tasks (coding, reasoning, planning, tool use) on which agents are evaluated and may fail.

nodes.name: Tool Use
nodes.description: One of the capability dimensions used to explain agent performance in the model-capability framing. | 软件工程能力的关键组成之一，指使用各类外部工具完成工程任务。

nodes.name: Planning
nodes.description: One of the capability dimensions used to explain agent performance in the model-capability framing.

nodes.name: Reasoning
nodes.description: One of the capability dimensions used to explain agent performance in the model-capability framing.

nodes.name: Coding
nodes.description: One of the capability dimensions used to explain agent performance in the model-capability framing.

nodes.name: Development Harness
nodes.description: A runtime resource management layer analogous to a traditional operating system, but designed for managing agent runtime resources such as context, tools, memory, and verification. | 开发用的 agent harness 框架，包含属性 P4（归因先于恢复）和 P5（维护与熵感知）等设计原则，并枚举了 11 个组件职责。

nodes.name: Operating System
nodes.description: Traditional operating systems that manage CPU, memory, files, processes, and devices, serving as the structural analogy for the development harness.

nodes.name: Context Budget
nodes.description: A runtime resource managed by the development harness, representing the available context window for the agent. | Context trace 所捕获的运行时资源，约束上下文长度 | 运行时资源之一，代表代理可以查看和推理的内容范围。未管理时会导致选错文件、遗漏约束。

nodes.name: Tool Budget
nodes.description: A runtime resource managed by the development harness, constraining the agent's tool usage. | Tool trace 所捕获的运行时资源，约束工具调用次数 | 运行时资源之一，代表代理可以执行的操作及其时机。未管理时会导致无法检查、测试或修改。

nodes.name: Verification Evidence
nodes.description: A runtime resource managed by the development harness, providing proof of agent actions and outcomes. | 运行时资源之一，代表需求已被满足的证明。未管理时会导致过早地宣称成功。

nodes.name: Project Memory
nodes.description: A runtime resource managed by the development harness, persisting project-level state across agent operations. | AI Development Harness 中用于持久化存储项目级记忆信息的组件。 | 软件工程活动中需要的关键能力之一，用于在长周期任务中保留项目级记忆。 | Context trace 所捕获的运行时资源，存储项目级记忆信息 | A component of the agent harness that maintains persistent memory across an agent's tasks. | 为 agent 提供的项目级记忆，仅提供 memory 不足以带来收益，还需要可被检查地使用。

nodes.name: Task State
nodes.description: A runtime resource managed by the development harness, tracking the current state of agent tasks. | AI Development Harness 中用于追踪任务执行状态的组件。 | A component of the agent harness that tracks the current state of ongoing tasks.

nodes.name: Human Attention
nodes.description: A runtime resource managed by the development harness, representing the human's cognitive bandwidth available to the agent.

nodes.name: Permission Boundary
nodes.description: A runtime resource managed by the development harness, defining the security and access constraints for the agent.

nodes.name: Failure Signal
nodes.description: A runtime resource managed by the development harness, surfacing errors and failures from agent operations.

nodes.name: Entropy Budget
nodes.description: A runtime resource managed by the development harness, regulating randomness or exploratory behavior of the agent.

nodes.name: Test-Time Compute
nodes.description: A runtime resource managed by the development harness, governing computation available during agent inference.

nodes.name: harness framework
nodes.description: 为智能体提供运行时支持配置的词汇表的框架，本身不直接支持实证探究。 | 提出未来仓库需要具备智能体可读性的框架性建议，倡导将架构、测试实践、测试失败解读等显式化

nodes.name: controlled harness ladder
nodes.description: 提出的 H0-H3 四级渐进式运行时支持梯，用于在固定任务、仓库和模型条件下可控地改变运行时支持。

nodes.name: H0-H3 ladder
nodes.description: controlled harness ladder 的具体四个等级（H0 至 H3），逐步向智能体暴露运行时支持。

nodes.name: runtime support
nodes.description: 暴露给智能体的运行时支持配置以及执行过程中产生的证据，是研究对象的核心维度。 | The surrounding infrastructure and tooling that an agent relies on during execution, whose adequacy is analyzed via the failure taxonomy.

nodes.name: empirical inquiry
nodes.description: 在 harness 框架基础上进行实证研究的活动，需要受控的运行时变量手段。

nodes.name: Evaluating LLMs Trained on Code
nodes.description: 论文《Evaluating large language models trained on code》(arXiv:2107.03374),由 Mark Chen 等人于 2021 年发表,提出了 HumanEval 基准用于评估代码大语言模型

nodes.name: Mark Chen
nodes.description: OpenAI 研究员,论文《Evaluating large language models trained on code》的第一作者 | First author of the GPT-4 Technical Report (reference [1]).

nodes.name: Tom B. Brown
nodes.description: OpenAI 研究员,论文《Language models are few-shot learners》(GPT-3)的第一作者

nodes.name: Current plan
nodes.description: The active plan, inspected files, and open questions tracked during agent execution

nodes.name: Drift
nodes.description: Failure mode where agent execution deviates incoherently from the intended plan | 智能体在长程任务中出现的目标偏移、重复劳动与不一致问题

nodes.name: Human attention
nodes.description: Cost of human oversight and intervention required during agent episodes | Cost of human assistance during an agent episode; high cost is associated with high missing-harness intervention rate.

nodes.name: Permission boundary
nodes.description: Definition of allowed and forbidden actions for the agent to prevent unsafe operations | Defines allowed actions/scopes for the agent within a harness; a core constraint category in agent execution. | 限制高风险操作并暴露审批门的权限边界机制

nodes.name: Failure signal
nodes.description: Structured feedback obtained from tests, logs, and runtime for diagnosing agent failures

nodes.name: Entropy budget
nodes.description: Budget tracking the maintenance burden introduced by the agent over time | 衡量 agent 引入的维护负担的概念，与长期退化相关。

nodes.name: Test-time compute
nodes.description: Compute resources allocated for verification and exploration during agent execution | 用于验证与探索的测试阶段计算开销，可能引发运行命令失控与高成本循环。

nodes.name: Poor recovery
nodes.description: Failure mode characterized by random patching and inability to recover from errors

nodes.name: Long-term degradation
nodes.description: Failure mode where agent performance or behavior degrades over extended periods

nodes.name: AI Harness
nodes.description: 本文提出的形式化概念，将运行时底座（runtime substrate）定义为 AI Harness，用于中介基础模型代理对项目的观察、行动、反馈与变更完成确认。

nodes.name: harness
nodes.description: 作为运行时底座的运行时子层（runtime substrate），中介基础模型智能体如何观察项目、对其采取行动、接收反馈并确认变更已完成。 | 围绕模型的运行时支撑组件，其贡献需在 H0-H3 harness ladder 中与模型贡献分离。 | A wrapping/coordinating layer around LLM-based coding agents whose internal components can be exposed, hidden, ablated, traced, and compared, and which produces structured evidence of agent behavior. | 软件开发工程导向的智能体底座（substrate），用于塑造智能体行为，是智能体通过工具执行动作的组件之一 | The orchestration infrastructure around the model, whose effects need to be separated from model capability | 一种用于管理 Agent 行为使之保持一致性、可验证和可维护性的概念框架，区别于研究对象本身。 | 在 Agent 系统中，harness 是位于模型外部的任务运行时基础设施，用于治理 Agent 如何观察项目、对其执行操作、接收反馈以及确定完成条件；其组件可被暴露、隐藏、消融、追踪和比较，并能产出可评估的证据。 | 在 harness–environment 系统中作为中介底层（mediating substrate）的核心组件，连接模型与运行环境。 | The mediating substrate within the model–harness–environment system through which runtime operations are channeled and system behavior is determined. | A runtime support structure surrounding autonomous software engineering episodes; previously acknowledged by practitioners but not formally treated as a research object. | 运行时配置机制，治理整个 episode（会话/任务全程）的资源调度与管理，是本文提出的核心类比概念。 | A runtime wrapper/framework for AI agents that satisfies five design principles (P1-P5) including explicit runtime resources, traceable mediation, and requirement-level verification. | 智能体运行时承担各项责任（如文件检查、测试输出解读、变更验证、证据记录）的支撑组件/机制 | A software engineering agent evaluation framework whose facets are illuminated by different task classes and whose effects may interact with model capability. | 运行时配置（runtime configuration），提供暴露给软件智能体的支持集合，用于治理一个完整的 episode（情节）。 | The component that shapes how an agent selects context, uses tools, interprets failures, verifies behavior, and avoids maintenance burden.

nodes.name: software-engineering agents
nodes.description: 自治软件工程智能体，在真实开发环境中仍被认为不可靠的问题对象。

nodes.name: model-harness-environment system
nodes.description: 本文提出的系统视角，认为软件工程能力涌现于模型-底座-环境这一整体系统中，而非单纯取决于模型能力。 | The composite system of model, harness, and environment that is expected to produce auditable evidence that task requirements are satisfied.

nodes.name: foundation-model agent
nodes.description: 作为系统核心的基础模型智能体，其对项目的观察、行动与反馈均由 harness 底座中介。 | 基于基础模型的智能体，其行为特性使传统的人类导向软件仓库设计假设失效

nodes.name: Trace schemas
nodes.description: 线结构化的 JSON (JSONL) 跟踪模式定义，用于记录代理运行时的资源使用与失败类型信息。

nodes.name: Action trace
nodes.description: 记录代理外部有意义操作的跟踪，包括 read_file、edit_file、run_tool、write_report、update_task_state、inspect_diff、declare_complete 等动作。 | 记录智能体操作序列（agent operations）的追踪类型，关联整体回合连贯性（overall episode coherence）。

nodes.name: declare_complete
nodes.description: Action trace 中记录的一种操作类型，表示代理声明任务完成。

nodes.name: update_task_state
nodes.description: Action trace 中记录的一种操作类型，用于更新任务状态。

nodes.name: H0-H3 harness ladder
nodes.description: 受控可见性消融框架，向智能体逐步暴露更多的运行时支持，使 harness 的贡献与模型的贡献在经验上可分离。

nodes.name: trace-based evaluation protocol
nodes.description: 基于轨迹的评估协议，记录八类执行证据（action、tool、context、verification、failure attribution、intervention、entropy、outcome），以验证自主性而非任务成功作为裁决依据。

nodes.name: verification autonomy
nodes.description: 作为智能体单次运行裁决依据的评估准则，强调对验证自主能力的衡量。 | A quantitative metric measuring how autonomously a model can verify its outputs | A process-level metric capturing the degree to which verification proceeds without external assistance. | 验证自主性指标，用于评估系统在不依赖外部人工干预下完成验证的能力。

nodes.name: eight classes of execution evidence
nodes.description: trace-based evaluation protocol 所记录八类执行证据：action、tool、context、verification、failure attribution、intervention、entropy、outcome。

nodes.name: H0–H3 ladder
nodes.description: A multi-level evaluation framework referenced with rules R3–R5 and an operational definition given by the visibility matrix. | A hierarchical ladder (H0–H3) used to classify evidence structure of episode packages, shown to be operationally feasible through a controlled validation task. | A hypothesis-to-evidence escalation ladder (H0 through H3) referenced as part of an episode packaging framework, shown to be operationally feasible. | 一种分级评估框架（H0–H3），衡量智能体是否检查相关上下文、使用工具、运行测试、复现失败、归因、验证每项需求、保持先验行为、避免无关修改、引入熵及需要人工介入等行为。 | A set of hypotheses (H0–H3) used in the study, for which repoA serves as the supporting empirical artifact. | A hypothesis ladder (H0 through H3) that repoA is built to support. | A multi-level framework where artifact visibility increases monotonically across levels H0, H1, H2, and H3.

nodes.name: Runtime support (R3)
nodes.description: Requirement stating that when a level provides a capability, its use is recorded.

nodes.name: No hidden evaluator leakage (R4)
nodes.description: Requirement that expected files, expected fixes, and evaluator notes are not visible to the agent at any level.

nodes.name: Outcome comparability (R5)
nodes.description: Requirement that every level is adjudicated under the same final outcome taxonomy.

nodes.name: Visibility matrix
nodes.description: Table 3 specifying which artifacts are visible at each H0–H3 level; serves as the operational definition of the ladder. | Table 3 that specifies which artifacts are visible (✓) or hidden (—) at each level of the H0–H3 ladder; serves as the operational definition of the ladder.

nodes.name: Final outcome taxonomy
nodes.description: Unified outcome classification scheme applied across all levels of the H0–H3 ladder to ensure comparability.

nodes.name: AI Development Harness
nodes.description: A framework that mediates runtime resources analogous to conventional operating systems, managing agent context, memory, and verification. | 围绕 Foundation Model 构建的开发框架，包含任务接口、上下文管理、工具注册表、项目记忆、任务状态、可观测性层、失败归因与验证协议等核心组件。 | 用于软件开发的AI开发框架体系，由提示、agent框架、ACIs、DevOps工具等构建而成，包含11个组件职责。 | 一个AI开发框架，负责管理AI代理的运行时资源，类似于但又区别于传统操作系统的资源管理方式。 | A runtime interface specifically focused on shaping the behavior of foundation-model agents within software-development environments, distinct from DevOps and platform engineering.

nodes.name: H1
nodes.description: A baseline level of AI Development Harness that provides agent-readable project knowledge and verification discipline. | Harness level 1, which additionally receives tool-related artifacts beyond H0. | Outcome level producing unverified_success: produces patch, tool trace, targeted login test, lint evidence, while full regression records a timeout. | First verification hypothesis requiring deterministic checks pass, patch, tool trace, targeted login test, lint, and full regression records to unverified_success; associated with a timeout

nodes.name: H2
nodes.description: Context–memory harness built on H1, adding agent-readable project memory, a task-state file, and a context-selection protocol to make context use explicit and traceable. | Agent level/configuration that receives an agent guide, architecture document, testing guide, task-state file, known-failures file, and context-selection protocol. | Outcome level producing unverified_success that builds on H1 evidence (H1 evidence plus additional content). | Second verification hypothesis requiring H1 evidence plus context trace over project memory and updated task state to achieve unverified_success | A verification harness level of the ladder, consisting of a deterministic behavioral check registry, bug-reproduction protocol, failure-attribution protocol, verification protocol, and verification report template. | The runtime-support level that H3 extends; serves as the base layer for the H3 observability–verification harness.

nodes.name: agent-readable project memory
nodes.description: Structured project knowledge covering architecture, testing conventions, and known failures that is consumable by agents.

nodes.name: task-state file
nodes.description: A file used by the H2 harness to track and manage task state explicitly.

nodes.name: context-selection protocol
nodes.description: A protocol in the H2 harness that governs how context is selected, making context use explicit and traceable. | Protocol introduced at H2 for selecting relevant context to provide to the agent.

nodes.name: verification discipline
nodes.description: A discipline enforced by the H1 harness to ensure agent outputs and behaviors are verified.

nodes.name: Continuous Delivery
nodes.description: 由 Jez Humble 和 David Farley 合著的经典著作《Continuous Delivery: Reliable Software Releases through Build, Test, and Deployment Automation》，2010 年由 Addison-Wesley 出版，提出持续交付的软件工程方法论。 | 持续交付是一种软件工程方法论，确保软件随时可以可靠地发布到生产环境。 | Book titled 'Continuous Delivery: Reliable Software Releases through Build, Test, and Deployment Automation' by Jez Humble and David Farley, published by Addison-Wesley in 2010.

nodes.name: The DevOps Handbook
nodes.description: 由 Gene Kim、Jez Humble、Patrick Debois 和 John Willis 合著的 DevOps 实践指南，2016 年由 IT Revolution Press 出版。

nodes.name: Jez Humble
nodes.description: DevOps 与持续交付领域的知名作者，《Continuous Delivery》和《The DevOps Handbook》的共同作者。 | Co-author of the book 'Continuous Delivery' (Addison-Wesley, 2010).

nodes.name: David Farley
nodes.description: 《Continuous Delivery》的共同作者，倡导持续交付与自动化测试部署实践。 | Co-author of the book 'Continuous Delivery' (Addison-Wesley, 2010).

nodes.name: Gene Kim
nodes.description: 《The DevOps Handbook》的共同作者，DevOps 运动的推动者之一。 | Author mentioned in a truncated reference following the Continuous Delivery entry.

nodes.name: Patrick Debois
nodes.description: 《The DevOps Handbook》的共同作者，DevOps 一词的提出者之一。

nodes.name: John Willis
nodes.description: 《The DevOps Handbook》的共同作者，DevOps 领域的实践者与布道者。

nodes.name: DevOps
nodes.description: 融合开发（Development）与运维（Operations）的软件工程方法论，强调自动化、持续交付与协作。 | 为人类与机器开发工作流提供基础设施的实践，被明确区别于智能体开发 harness | Provides infrastructure for human and machine development workflows; explicitly distinguished from a development harness.

nodes.name: entropy auditor
nodes.description: A mechanism within autonomous engineering that analyzes and addresses project residue such as redundant code, stale documentation, unnecessary dependencies, weakened tests, debug scripts, inconsistent task notes, and architecture violations. | AI Harness 中用于审计熵（不确定性/混乱度）的关键组件，缺失时会触发人工干预。

nodes.name: autonomous agents
nodes.description: Agents that not only produce solutions but also generate residue (redundant code, stale docs, unnecessary dependencies, weakened tests, etc.) that degrades projects over time.

nodes.name: autonomous engineering
nodes.description: Engineering paradigm where autonomous agents handle development tasks; entropy is treated as an inherent part of this process.

nodes.name: entropy
nodes.description: The cumulative degradation in a project caused by residue such as redundant code, stale documentation, unnecessary dependencies, weakened tests, debug scripts, inconsistent task notes, or architecture violations.

nodes.name: tool stability
nodes.description: Reframed as an analyzable runtime resource rather than an incidental engineering nuisance, enabling systematic analysis within autonomous engineering.

nodes.name: project residue
nodes.description: Byproducts of autonomous agent activity including redundant code, stale documentation, unnecessary dependencies, weakened tests, debug scripts, inconsistent task notes, and architecture violations that do not break immediate tasks but degrade the project over time.

nodes.name: entropy management
nodes.description: 在软件工程中，与代码变更同等重要的概念，涉及管理 AI 生成代码带来的复杂度和不确定性累积

nodes.name: AI-native development environments
nodes.description: 面向基础模型智能体（foundation-model agent）设计的未来代码仓库形态，需要显式的、可被智能体读取的架构与约定

nodes.name: software repositories
nodes.description: 当前为人类开发者设计的代码仓库，依赖读者根据惯例推断架构、记忆测试实践等人类能力

nodes.name: Context Trace
nodes.description: Records every project-memory artifact consulted by the agent with a structured contribution field. | Record of the model's context (inputs, prompts, retrieved information) over the course of an agent episode. | 捕获 context budget 和 project memory 等运行时资源的追踪类型，用于诊断 context 类失败 | 运行时记录机制，记录 agent 查阅了哪个 memory artifact、其贡献以及是否影响了决策，使 memory 从不可见的 prompt 成分变为可分析的运行时资源。

nodes.name: Verification Trace
nodes.description: Records every verification attempt with type, method, result, covered requirements, and the agent's interpretation. | Record of verification steps performed during an agent episode to check correctness of intermediate outputs. | 记录验证尝试的类型、方法、结果、所覆盖的需求及智能体解释的追踪机制，支持包括 bug 复现、确定性行为检查、注册测试、定向测试、全量回归、lint、补丁审查和人工评估检查等多种验证类型。

nodes.name: Failure-Attribution Log
nodes.description: Log component for attributing failures within the agent's execution trace system. | 记录失败归因信息的日志组件，包含观察输出、期望输出、失败类型、证据、替代解释以及下一步诊断动作。 | A structured log required only at hierarchy level H3 that attributes failures to identified causes.

nodes.name: Project-Memory Artifact
nodes.description: Memory items consulted by the agent, recorded by the context trace.

nodes.name: Verification Attempt
nodes.description: An agent's check operation whose details are captured by the verification trace.

nodes.name: Evaluation Pipeline
nodes.description: A pipeline for evaluating agent systems, comprising input pack, agent episode execution, episode package generation, and outcome classification stages (as depicted in Figure 3). | The end-to-end pipeline that converts an input pack through an agent episode into an episode package and classifies it via a five-label outcome taxonomy.

nodes.name: Input Pack
nodes.description: The initial artifact bundle for an evaluation episode, composed of task, repository, and harness artifacts. | The input artifact bundle to the evaluation pipeline, consisting of task, repository, and harness artifacts.

nodes.name: Agent Episode
nodes.description: A single evaluation run in which the model acts under a harness, distinct from a single model response; the unit of analysis at the episode level rather than turn level. | The execution process by which an input pack is converted into an episode package during the evaluation pipeline.

nodes.name: Harness
nodes.description: The execution environment under which an agent model acts during an evaluation episode. | A layer that surrounds a foundation-model software agent and manages context, tools, project memory, task state, observability, failure attribution, verification, permissions, and maintenance state. | Runtime scaffolding that supplies support a human would otherwise have to provide; a harness that lowers M-HIR is more complete. | 代码执行与交互的承载环境，具有不同层级（levels），长期评估在不同 harness 层级下运行以测试其对维护负担的影响。 | 承载自主智能体运行的测试/执行框架，工具稳定性被视为其核心工程问题。 | 包裹 agent 的执行环境/基础设施组件，H3 将验证能力内置其中，使 agent 可以在该环境中进行自验证。 | The evaluation harness providing artifacts, allowed tools, intervention policy, and verification procedure that frame an episode. | The execution environment or loop in which autonomous agents operate; the entropy auditor is placed inside it.

nodes.name: Intervention Log
nodes.description: Log of human or system interventions during an agent episode, captured in the episode package. | 记录人类协助干预信息的日志组件。 | Records every human action along with an avoidability classification and a corresponding harness-gap label.

nodes.name: Outcome Record
nodes.description: Final outcome documentation of an agent episode, including patch and verification report.

nodes.name: Outcome Classification
nodes.description: Classification stage applying a 5-label taxonomy to categorize the outcome of each agent episode. | The process of classifying an episode package using the five-label taxonomy to produce a final outcome.

nodes.name: 5-label Taxonomy
nodes.description: A taxonomy of five outcome labels used to classify the results of agent episodes in the evaluation pipeline. | The five-label final-outcome classification scheme applied to the episode package to determine its evaluation outcome.

nodes.name: Agent Failure Taxonomy
nodes.description: A taxonomy categorizing agent runtime failures into distinct types to enable analysis beyond simple pass/fail evaluation.

nodes.name: Ffeedback
nodes.description: Failure category where feedback is unavailable or not interpretable. | 失败类型之一：反馈不可用或不可解释。

nodes.name: Fverify
nodes.description: Failure category where the agent cannot prove the task requirements are satisfied. | 失败类型之一：智能体无法证明任务需求已被满足。

nodes.name: Frecovery
nodes.description: Failure category where the agent cannot recover from a failure. | 失败类型之一：智能体无法从失败中恢复。

nodes.name: Fentropy
nodes.description: Failure category where the agent introduces maintenance burden (entropic decay). | 失败类型之一（原文被截断），与智能体的熵增/退化相关。

nodes.name: Fmodel
nodes.description: Failure category attributed to model reasoning or coding failure despite adequate harness and environment.

nodes.name: Funknown
nodes.description: Failure category for failures that cannot be confidently attributed to any other class.

nodes.name: pass/fail evaluation
nodes.description: Traditional binary evaluation method that cannot explain what kind of runtime support is missing when an agent fails.

nodes.name: SWE-agent
nodes.description: 提出 Agent-Computer Interfaces 概念，实现自动化软件工程的研究，发表于 NeurIPS 2024 | 发表于 NeurIPS 2024 的自动化软件工程智能体框架，通过 agent-computer interfaces 实现自动化软件工程。

nodes.name: OpenHands
nodes.description: 一个面向 AI 代理的开源开放平台，由 Xingyao Wang、Graham Neubig 等人开发 | An open platform for AI software developers as generalist agents, published at ICLR 2025.

nodes.name: Xingyao Wang
nodes.description: OpenHands 论文的第一作者

nodes.name: Graham Neubig
nodes.description: OpenHands 论文的通讯/资深作者 | Co-author of OpenHands and affiliated researcher in the cited author list.

nodes.name: Foundation-model software agent
nodes.description: A software agent built on foundation models, which is wrapped and managed by a harness layer.

nodes.name: agent-computer interfaces
nodes.description: A related but distinct research area referenced as prior work [4], distinguished from AI Harness Engineering.

nodes.name: agent frameworks
nodes.description: Existing agent frameworks referenced as prior work [11, 12], distinguished from AI Harness Engineering as a new research object.

nodes.name: Missing-harness human intervention
nodes.description: A type of human intervention made when the system is missing an entropy auditor in its harness.

nodes.name: M-HIR
nodes.description: Missing-harness human intervention rate, defined as the ratio of missing-harness interventions to total episodes, measuring how much runtime support the harness fails to supply. | A quantitative metric defined in the evaluation protocol, likely measuring model-harness interaction rate | missing-harness human intervention rate，衡量因 Harness 缺失组件而导致的人工干预率。 | Missing-Harness Human Intervention Rate (M-HIR)，衡量缺失 harness 情况下人工干预比率的度量指标。

nodes.name: Entropy auditor
nodes.description: A runtime component of the harness that, when absent, triggers a missing-harness human intervention. | A component that detects maintenance burden introduced by the agent, operating over permission records to identify invalid episodes, stale docs, dependency churn, and residue. | 检测 Agent 引入的维护负担（如陈旧文档、依赖变更、残留物）的审计器

nodes.name: Failure taxonomy
nodes.description: An eight-category classification (e.g., Fcontext, …) used to distinguish failure types when treating the system as the unit of analysis. | 一种将系统作为分析单位的诊断方法论，依赖于区分不同的失败类型来提供诊断价值。

nodes.name: System
nodes.description: Treated as the unit of analysis in the diagnostic framework, whose failures are classified by the failure taxonomy.

nodes.name: Task Interface
nodes.description: AI Development Harness 中用于定义和接收任务的人机交互接口组件。 | AI Development Harness的11个组件职责之一，负责呈现目标、需求、约束和成功标准。

nodes.name: Context Manager
nodes.description: AI Development Harness 中用于管理智能体运行时上下文信息的组件。

nodes.name: Tool Registry
nodes.description: AI Development Harness 中用于注册与管理可用工具的组件。

nodes.name: Observability
nodes.description: AI Development Harness 中的可观测性层，用于支撑对智能体行为的监控；当缺失时需由人工解读测试失败。 | Harness 结构中的关键模块，用于代理行为的可观测性。 | A capability embedded in the harness for monitoring agent behavior and runtime activity. | Harness Ladder 中受 UI-behavior Tasks 压力测试的可观测性能力

nodes.name: Verification Protocol
nodes.description: AI Development Harness 中用于自动验证智能体最终行为正确性的协议组件。 | Protocol for verification provided to the H3-level agent configuration. | A protocol within H2 governing how verification is performed on task outputs. | Harness Ladder 中受 Validation Tasks 压力测试的验证协议组件

nodes.name: runtime structure
nodes.description: 中介模型每一步操作的运行时结构/脚手架，其存在与设计质量决定了系统能否表现得像模型具备能力 | The mediating structure through which every step of a task is processed; when present and well-designed the system appears competent, when absent or unstable the same model appears incompetent.

nodes.name: task representation
nodes.description: 模型接收到的任务表征，是模型执行任务的起点

nodes.name: repository observation
nodes.description: 模型对仓库的子集观察，作为其感知输入的一部分

nodes.name: tools
nodes.description: 模型通过接口调用的工具，用于执行具体操作 | 智能体通过其执行动作的机制，是 harness 的组成要素之一

nodes.name: interface
nodes.description: 模型调用工具所经过的接口层

nodes.name: feedback
nodes.description: 来自测试或命令的反馈，模型据此判断任务是否完成

nodes.name: agent system
nodes.description: 由模型与运行时结构共同构成的智能体系统，其表现取决于运行时结构的设计

nodes.name: episode
nodes.description: 对模型行为的一次评估单元，失败归因于模型，成功则归功于模型 | 一次完整的运行/会话周期，是 harness 所治理的作用粒度单位。 | A defined unit of execution/interaction within the evaluation protocol (being formally specified in this section).

nodes.name: empty-string password defect
nodes.description: A defect where the system accepts empty-string passwords, used as a benchmark target.

nodes.name: deterministic behavioral probes
nodes.description: Three objectively checkable behavioral probes used to verify the defect.

nodes.name: structured workflow
nodes.description: A workflow used to verify the fix for the defect.

nodes.name: harness instantiation
nodes.description: The process of instantiating harness levels by exposing specific artifacts listed in Table 3.

nodes.name: H0
nodes.description: Harness level 0, instantiated with a task description file and the unmodified repository tree. | Highest outcome level in the hierarchy producing autonomous_verified_success: evaluator-side deterministic checks pass and full regression succeeds. | 基线对比点（baseline comparison point），无工具注册表、无项目记忆、无验证协议，所有其他层次的对照基准。

nodes.name: architectural layer
nodes.description: The specific architectural layer that must be modified to fix the empty-string password defect; fixing elsewhere would be a wrong-layer fix.

nodes.name: Table 3
nodes.description: Reference table listing the artifacts exposed for each harness level instantiation.

nodes.name: Patch Verification Taxonomy
nodes.description: A classification taxonomy that maps software patch requirements to evidence, distinguishing task behavior from evidence quality across multiple outcome categories.

nodes.name: autonomous_verified_success
nodes.description: Patch outcome category where the patch passes deterministic checks while using an internal verification protocol that maps requirements to evidence. | Final outcome achieved at H0 level, where evaluator-side deterministic checks pass and full regression succeeds. | Outcome label assigned by rule to a patch that passes all deterministic checks and is accompanied by a verification protocol mapping requirements to evidence. | Outcome label indicating that task requirements are satisfied and sufficient evidence is produced without missing-harness human intervention. | 评估分类：任务需求满足并产生充分证据，且无人工干预辅助下的成功状态。 | 一种验证成功状态指标，仅在确定性需求覆盖完整且限制已在验证报告中报告的前提下达成

nodes.name: unverified_success
nodes.description: Patch outcome category where the patch passes deterministic checks without an internal verification protocol. | Final outcome achieved at H1 and H2 levels, lacking the structured verification provided by the H3 protocol. | Outcome label assigned to a patch that passes deterministic checks but lacks an internal verification protocol. | 评估分类：补丁看似正确或任务行为通过评估端检查，但代理本身未根据协议产生充分证据的状态。

nodes.name: failed
nodes.description: Patch outcome category representing an unsuccessful patch. | 评估分类：所需行为失败、补丁导致测试失败或无可用结果的失败状态。

nodes.name: unsafe_invalid
nodes.description: Patch outcome category where the patch weakens tests or introduces unrelated destructive edits. | Patch taxonomy category representing patches that are unsafe or invalid according to the classification scheme. | 评估结果分类之一，表示测试被削弱、出现无关破坏性编辑，或任务被绕过。

nodes.name: assisted_verified_success
nodes.description: Patch outcome category for a successful patch that required substantive human assistance. | Patch taxonomy category representing successful patches that required substantive human assistance during verification. | Outcome label indicating that the final patch is correct but required (key) human intervention, alongside verified results. | 评估分类：最终补丁正确，但关键进展或验证依赖人工协助的成功状态。

nodes.name: Task Behavior
nodes.description: One of the two dimensions distinguished by the patch verification taxonomy, describing what the patch does.

nodes.name: Evidence Quality
nodes.description: One of the two dimensions distinguished by the patch verification taxonomy, describing how well the patch is verified.

nodes.name: Deterministic Checks
nodes.description: Verification mechanism used to evaluate whether a patch is successful. | Short commands that exercise corrected behavior, preserved valid-input behavior, and preserved invalid-input behavior with expected output substrings; serve dual roles as harness artifacts and adjudication checks.

nodes.name: Internal Verification Protocol
nodes.description: Protocol within the patch that maps requirements to evidence, used to determine autonomous_verified_success versus unverified_success.

nodes.name: Full Regression Handling
nodes.description: Mechanism for handling full regression (text truncated in chunk).

nodes.name: Intervention logger
nodes.description: A component that records human assistance and its avoidability, surfacing the invisible human scaffolding behind agent operation. | 记录人工协助行为的日志器，弥补 Agent 自动修复能力的不足

nodes.name: Permission record
nodes.description: An evidence artifact recording invalid episodes during agent execution, serving as input to the entropy auditor. | 权限边界的执行记录，用于审计不安全或无效的操作片段

nodes.name: Entropy audit
nodes.description: The audit process/output produced by the entropy auditor to detect maintenance burden such as stale docs, dependency churn, and residue. | 熵审计器的输出结果，揭示 Agent 运行过程中累积的技术债务 | 熵审计组件，管理熵预算（entropy budget）。

nodes.name: Intervention log
nodes.description: The log artifact produced by the intervention logger, recording human interventions and whether they were avoidable. | 人工介入日志，捕获人类注意力（human attention）信号，作为诊断信号贯穿所有 trace 类型。

nodes.name: Invisible human scaffolding
nodes.description: The concept describing undisclosed human assistance that sustains agent operation, made visible through intervention logs.

nodes.name: Maintenance burden
nodes.description: The accumulated operational cost (stale docs, dependency churn, residue) introduced by an agent that the entropy auditor detects.

nodes.name: Harness framework
nodes.description: A framework providing a vocabulary for runtime supports exposed to the agent and the evidence produced during execution.

nodes.name: Controlled harness ladder
nodes.description: A graduated harness configuration methodology layered on top of the harness framework's vocabulary.

nodes.name: Software Engineering
nodes.description: 被描述为长周期、有状态、工具中介、反馈驱动的活动，与模型能力提升是当前代理研究的另一关键维度。

nodes.name: Context Management
nodes.description: 软件工程活动中需要的关键能力之一，用于处理和维护任务上下文。 | Harness 结构中的关键模块，用于管理代理的上下文。

nodes.name: Tool Interfaces
nodes.description: 软件工程活动中需要的工具交互接口能力。

nodes.name: Execution Traces
nodes.description: 软件工程活动中需要的执行轨迹记录与追踪能力。

nodes.name: Validation Signals
nodes.description: 软件工程活动中需要的来自执行结果的验证反馈信号。

nodes.name: Agent Loops
nodes.description: 通过组合更优模型形成的更复杂的代理循环结构。

nodes.name: Framework
nodes.description: The overarching framework discussed in this context, which integrates verification and attribution as core responsibilities. | The unnamed framework illustrated on a controlled task, featuring a H0–H3 evidence ladder and a protocol that yields inspectable episode packages. | The overall system comprising the framework, the ladder, and the protocol, which together support verifiable, attributed, and maintainable autonomous software engineering changes.

nodes.name: Ladder
nodes.description: A hierarchical concept (ladder of levels) within the framework, where lower levels treat completion as an assertion and higher levels bind agents to produce requirement-linked evidence. | One of the three pillars (framework, ladder, protocol) that together support reframing autonomous software engineering.

nodes.name: Proto
nodes.description: A protocol-level concept referenced alongside the framework and ladder, likely defining interaction or communication norms.

nodes.name: H3
nodes.description: A specific level in the framework's ladder that binds the agent to produce requirement-linked evidence, distinguishing a verifiable change from a mere patch. | Agent level/configuration that builds upon H2 and additionally receives a deterministic check registry, bug-reproduction protocol, failure-attribution protocol, verification protocol, and verification report template. | A system/framework that binds an agent to a five-step verification discipline, accommodating failure rather than hiding it. | A framework/method that inserts an attribution step between failed observations and corrective edits in agent systems, improving error handling beyond naive retry behavior. | A harness level whose distinctive contribution is converting task completion into a structured evidentiary object via a canonical workflow. | System/model context where a full-regression timeout does not by itself imply patch success. | A level/tier in a hierarchical evaluation framework where deterministic checks become agent-visible harness artifacts supporting the agent's own verification. | A component/hypothesis in the framework that binds the agent to produce requirement-linked evidence, distinguishing a verifiable change from a mere patch. | 四个 harness 等级之一，作为代理可访问确定性检查注册表的阈值等级 | A system whose distinctive contribution is to convert task completion into a structured evidentiary object via the canonical workflow: reproduce → attribute → fix → verify → report. | H3 verification workflow，将 agent 绑定到一个五步验证规程的组件 | Third verification hypothesis requiring H2 evidence plus bug reproduction log, failure attribution log, deterministic requirement checks, and a structured verification report explicitly recorded to achieve autonomous_verified_success | 一种包含归因（attribution）步骤的假设驱动编辑框架，智能体记录观察输出、期望输出、推断的失败类型、支持证据、替代解释及下一步诊断动作。 | A higher level of the ladder that makes task completion an evidentiary object rather than an assertion. | 一个将验证能力内置于 harness 中的框架/系统，强调 agent 必须能够复现失败、归因、施加有针对性的修复、检查每项需求并报告，从而产出可验证、可归因、可维护的变更。 | An evaluation level (likely Hierarchy Level 3) at which deterministic checks become agent-visible through the deterministic check registry. | The Observability–verification harness level; defined as H2 plus a deterministic behavioral check registry, bug-reproduction protocol, failure-attribution protocol, verification protocol, and verification report template. | A framework that places verification inside the harness, requiring the agent to reproduce failures, attribute them, apply targeted fixes, and report evidence. | 论文中的一个假设条件，规定在确定性需求覆盖完整时，full-regression 超时本身不阻止 autonomous_verified_success

nodes.name: Verification
nodes.description: A component responsibility within the framework that involves deterministic checks bound to specific requirements and can serve as a harness responsibility. | 对智能体生成的变更进行可验证性确认，是 harness engineering 所强调的核心属性之一。 | A runtime capability central to the framework's implication: shifting the question from producing a patch to producing a verifiable, attributed, maintainable change. | 执行三个确定性探针（deterministic probes）及定向测试的验证过程 | 一种运行时能力（runtime capability），用于检查并确认 agent 所声称的完成情况，区别于由人类或外部评估器执行的传统验证方式。 | A component of the agent harness that validates agent outputs and actions. | 软件工程能力的关键组成之一，指对修复或变更进行正确性验证。

nodes.name: Attribution
nodes.description: A component of the framework responsible for linking actions/outputs back to their source, with a back-edge from verification when initial diagnosis proves wrong. | 失败原因的归因层，是 H3 工作流中验证步骤可反向回退到的环节

nodes.name: Runtime systems for agent-first development
nodes.description: 以代理（agent）为优先开发对象的运行时系统范式，关注仓库设计、上下文选择与维护性等问题的研究框架。 | 一类专为基础模型智能体设计的运行时系统，类似操作系统但专注于管理上下文、工具、记忆、验证、权限、故障恢复、人为监督、熵、成本等

nodes.name: AI-native repository design
nodes.description: 面向 AI 代理可读性与可操作性的仓库设计理念，使文档结构、测试命令注册表与架构地图等仓库制品成为可被代理利用的一等研究对象。 | 一种面向 AI agent 可读性的仓库设计思路，主张仓库设计本身应成为研究问题，其结构需适配 harness 的使用。

nodes.name: agent-readable affordances
nodes.description: 仓库中可供 AI 代理读取与使用的提示性结构与接口特征，用于支持代理的上下文选择与行为恢复。

nodes.name: long-term maintenance burden

nodes.name: architecture-map format

nodes.name: repository artifacts
nodes.description: 仓库中作为开发输入输出的各类制品（文档、测试、架构图等），被该框架视为与模型和代理同等重要的研究对象。

nodes.name: recovery from instability
nodes.description: 代理在测试或执行过程中遭遇不稳定时恢复到正常状态的能力，由测试命令注册表的形态设计所支撑。

nodes.name: wrong-layer fixes
nodes.description: 代理在错误的架构层级上实施修复的现象，是 AI 原生仓库设计中需要通过架构地图格式来预防的问题。

nodes.name: Codex
nodes.description: OpenAI的编码代理，采用harness-like结构进行上下文管理、工具接口和反馈循环 | OpenAI 发布的面向智能体优先（agent-first）的软件工程实践/系统，源自 OpenAI 工程报告（2026）。

nodes.name: OpenAI

nodes.name: Microsoft

nodes.name: agent harness

nodes.description: 维护假设、已检视文件、未决问题与下一步行动，作为智能体执行任务的上下文状态 | Current plan, inspected files, and open questions maintained by the agent; missing it causes drift and incoherent execution. | 维护代理当前假设、已检视文件、未决问题与下一步任务状态 | H2 中维护的当前任务进行状态信息。

nodes.name: H3 Verification Workflow
nodes.description: The core five-step verification discipline of H3: Reproduce (observe failure vs. expected), Attribute (classify failure type & evidence), Fix (targeted edit to attributed layer), Verify (deterministic checks + preserved behavior), and Report (verification report + limits). | 五步验证工作流：复现失败、分类失败类型、应用针对性修复、验证行为、报告证据与限制，并包含从验证到归因的反向边

nodes.name: Re-attribution

nodes.name: Execution Evidence

nodes.name: Verification Autonomy
nodes.description: A judging criterion used by the framework to adjudicate agent runs, replacing task-success-only evaluation.

nodes.description: Tiered gradations of execution environment that systematically shape the evidence structure of episode packages.

nodes.name: Reproduction Logs

nodes.name: Structured Verification

nodes.name: Episode packages

nodes.name: Controlled validation task
nodes.description: A small, by-design task used to make the H0–H3 ladder and protocol concretely inspectable, not intended for population-level performance comparisons.

nodes.name: Repository cell

nodes.name: episode packages

nodes.name: login flow

nodes.name: human attention
nodes.description: A critical runtime resource representing the human-in-the-loop oversight channel available to a harness. | A diagnostic signal trace type capturing human attention allocation. | 人类介入或关注事件的诊断信号。
